Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions For Violating A Court Order And Misuse Of The Discovery Process Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Further Responses
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JOHN-PAUL S. DEOL, Esq. of Dhillon Law Group Inc. (Tel: 415-433-1700) for plaintiff Tayyaba Nazli;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: Plaintiff TAYYABA NAZLI's Motion For Sanctions For Violating A Court Order And Misuse Of The Discovery Process Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Further Responses.

Matter contested and argued only as to the setting of deadlines. Matter submitted.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling and adds the deadlines discussed at today's hearing, as follows:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses is granted in part. If they have not already done so, within 30 days of the Court's order, Defendants shall serve verified code-compliant further responses to Form Interrogatories - Economic (Set One) Nos. 211.2, 216.1 and 217.1. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions against Defendants and their counsel is granted in the amount of $1,500. Sanctions are to be paid within 30 days of the Court's order. Plaintiff's request for nonmonetary sanctions is denied at this time.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JOHN-PAUL S. DEOL, Esq. of Dhillon Law Group Inc. (Tel: 415-433-1700) for plaintiff Tayyaba Nazli;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: Plaintiff TAYYABA NAZLI's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Sanctions.

Matter contested and argued only as to the setting of deadlines. Matter submitted.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling and adds the deadlines discussed at today's hearing, as follows:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel further discovery responses is granted as set forth in the report and recommendation. Responses are due within 30 days of the Court's order. Defendants are awarded sanctions of $1,000 for plaintiff's failure to meet and confer and plaintiff is awarded $8,000 for defendants' failure to provide code-compliant responses. Thus, defendants and their counsel are ordered to pay plaintiff's counsel $7,000 within 30 days of this Court's order.

Order signed.
